data.title: OpenAI Alerts U.S. Lawmakers About DeepSeek's Tactics
data.description: OpenAI has issued a warning to U.S. lawmakers regarding its Chinese competitor, DeepSeek. Bloomberg posted on X that DeepSeek is allegedly employing unfair and advanced techniques to extract data from prominent U.S. AI models. This data is reportedly being used to train DeepSeek's upcoming R1 chatbot. OpenAI's concerns highlight the competitive landscape in the AI industry, where companies are striving to develop cutting-edge technologies. The situation underscores the need for regulatory oversight to ensure fair practices in the development and deployment of AI technologies.

data.title: DraftKings' 2026 Forecast Misses Wall Street Expectations
data.description: DraftKings, a prominent online betting company, released its sales and profit forecast for 2026, which did not meet Wall Street's expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that this announcement led to a decline in the company's stock during extended trading. The forecast has raised concerns among investors about the company's future performance and growth potential.

data.title: Scaramucci's Upcoming Interview Promises Revelations
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X about an upcoming interview with Anthony Scaramucci. Melker hinted at significant revelations, describing the interview as 'dropping bombs.' The exact content and details of the interview remain undisclosed, but anticipation is building for its release.

data.title: Trump Accounts to Offer Investment Opportunities for American Families
data.description: American parents will soon have the opportunity to open Trump Accounts for their children, providing new investment options. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing how these accounts function, who qualifies for financial benefits, and the potential advantages for participants. The program aims to support families by offering investment opportunities, with eligibility criteria determining access to free funds. This initiative is expected to impact numerous families across the United States, offering a new avenue for financial growth.

data.title: Australia Appoints New Member to Reserve Bank's Rate-Setting Committee Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description:
Australia's Treasurer, Jim Chalmers, has appointed Bruce Preston to the Reserve Bank of Australia's (RBA) rate-setting committee, replacing Alison Watkins whose term has ended. According to Jin10, Preston, an economics professor at the University of New South Wales and former senior advisor to both the RBA and the Treasury, will begin his five-year term on March 1. This appointment comes at a critical time for the RBA, as economic uncertainties intensify.

The RBA has been the first major central bank to raise interest rates this year due to persistent inflation. This week, both Governor Philip Lowe and Deputy Governor Guy Debelle have expressed a firm stance on inflation, indicating that further policy tightening may be necessary if price pressures prove to be deeply entrenched.

For global investors, the addition of a seasoned academic with central bank and treasury experience signals a commitment to maintaining technocratic decision-making continuity as Australia navigates external risks from weak productivity, a tight labor market, and broader geopolitical uncertainties.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Leads $3.5 Billion Funding for Clearwater Buyout
data.description: A consortium of private credit firms, spearheaded by Goldman Sachs Alternatives, is extending $3.5 billion to facilitate the acquisition of Clearwater. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this as a recent example of direct lenders playing a significant role in financing software company buyouts. This move underscores the growing influence of private credit firms in the financial landscape, particularly in the technology sector. The funding aims to support the strategic expansion and operational goals of Clearwater, reflecting a broader trend of private lenders stepping in where traditional banks might hesitate. This development is part of a larger pattern where direct lending is increasingly becoming a preferred option for financing large-scale acquisitions in the software industry.

data.title: Global Oil Prices Decline Amid Reduced Demand and Increased Supply Expectations
data.description: International oil prices fell nearly 3% due to decreased demand and anticipated supply increases, while concerns over renewed Middle East conflicts have diminished. According to RTHK, Brent crude oil in London closed at $67.52 per barrel, down $1.88, marking a 2.7% decrease. Meanwhile, New York crude oil settled at $62.84 per barrel, a decline of $1.79, or nearly 2.8%. The International Energy Agency (IEA) stated that global oil demand growth this year will be lower than previously forecasted. Despite supply disruptions in January leading to reduced production, the IEA estimates a significant surplus will still occur. Edited by Zhang Shimin.

data.title: Pinterest's Sales Forecast Misses Wall Street Expectations
data.description: Pinterest has announced its sales forecast for the current quarter, which did not meet Wall Street's expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that this comes shortly after the company revealed plans for layoffs and a strategic shift towards artificial intelligence products. The social media platform's recent moves indicate a significant transition in its business strategy as it adapts to changing market demands. Despite these efforts, the projected sales figures suggest challenges in meeting investor expectations.

data.title: Fiserv Introduces INDX for Real-Time Crypto Settlements
data.description: Fiserv, a prominent payments company, has unveiled INDX, a real-time cash payment system designed specifically for cryptocurrency firms. According to NS3.AI, this platform facilitates instant U.S. dollar settlements through a single custodial account and is operational 24/7. Additionally, INDX offers up to $25 million in insurance coverage, backed by FDIC protection.

data.title: Applied Materials Projects Strong Sales Amid AI and Memory Chip Demand
data.description: Applied Materials, the leading U.S. supplier of chipmaking equipment, has issued a positive sales forecast for the current quarter. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the company anticipates increased demand driven by artificial intelligence and memory semiconductors. This optimistic outlook suggests a robust market for chipmaking equipment as industries continue to invest in advanced technologies. The company's forecast reflects the growing importance of AI and memory chips in various sectors, contributing to a surge in equipment purchases. Applied Materials' projection underscores the ongoing expansion in the semiconductor industry, fueled by technological advancements and increased demand for high-performance computing solutions.

data.title: Airbnb Reports Strong Fourth-Quarter Bookings and Positive Revenue Outlook
data.description: Airbnb has reported robust bookings for the fourth quarter, attributing the success to strong travel demand and the increasing popularity of its new flexible payment and booking options. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's optimistic revenue forecast. The positive outlook reflects Airbnb's strategic efforts to adapt to evolving consumer preferences in the travel industry.

data.title: Oracle Secures $88 Million Cloud Contract with U.S. Air Force, Boosting Stock by 16%
data.description: Oracle has successfully obtained an $88 million cloud contract with the U.S. Air Force, strengthening its position in the Department of Defense's cloud modernization efforts. According to NS3.AI, this development has led to a 16% increase in Oracle's stock value this week, prompting Wall Street analysts to raise their price targets for the company. Despite differing opinions, several firms consider Oracle a promising investment in the software industry, with some price targets reaching as high as $400.

data.title: Westpac Reports First-Quarter Profit Increase Driven by Home Loans and Institutional Lending
data.description: Westpac Banking Corp. has reported a rise in its first-quarter profit, attributed to an increase in home loans and robust growth in institutional lending. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's performance in these key areas. The Australian bank's financial results reflect a positive trend in its lending activities, contributing to its overall profitability. This development comes as Westpac continues to focus on expanding its lending portfolio, particularly in the housing and institutional sectors. The bank's strategic emphasis on these areas has played a significant role in boosting its financial outcomes for the quarter.

data.title: Mixed Results for Major European Stock Markets
data.description: Major European stock markets closed with mixed results. According to RTHK, Germany's DAX index ended at 24,852 points, experiencing a slight decline of 3 points. Meanwhile, France's CAC index rose by 27 points to close at 8,340 points, marking an increase of 0.3%. In contrast, the UK's FTSE 100 index fell by 69 points, closing at 10,402 points, with a decrease of nearly 0.7%.

data.title: Rivian Anticipates Greater Losses Amid Cost Management Efforts
data.description: Rivian has cautioned that it might experience a larger-than-anticipated loss this year as it strives to manage expenses before the launch of its next-generation SUV. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the electric vehicle manufacturer in balancing cost control with its ambitious production goals. The company is focusing on streamlining operations to mitigate financial setbacks while preparing for the introduction of its new SUV model. Rivian's efforts to optimize its financial performance come at a crucial time as it seeks to strengthen its position in the competitive EV market.

data.title: Gallup Ends Longstanding Tradition of Tracking Presidential Approval Ratings
data.description: Gallup, a well-known American polling organization, announced on Wednesday, the 11th, that it will cease tracking presidential approval ratings, ending a tradition that has lasted for 88 years. According to Ming Pao, this decision follows a recent social media post by U.S. President Donald Trump, in which he criticized polling organizations, suggesting that 'false and misleading polls should be considered a criminal offense.'

data.title: FTC Warns Apple Over Alleged Suppression of Conservative Views on Apple News
data.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) issued a warning letter to Apple CEO Tim Cook on Wednesday, accusing the company's news platform, Apple News, of potentially suppressing conservative viewpoints, which may violate consumer protection laws. According to Ming Pao, FTC Chair Ferguson referenced a report by the conservative watchdog organization, Media Research Center (MRC), which highlighted that none of the 620 popular news stories pushed by Apple News in January originated from right-wing media outlets such as Fox News or the Daily Mail. Ferguson emphasized that if Apple is filtering content based on political bias, it could mislead consumers. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Chair Carl also expressed support for the FTC's stance, criticizing Apple for allegedly suppressing conservative perspectives.
